title: Vision Guide Programming - Overview
description:
Vision Guide Programming - Overview

Vision Guide Programming Overview

Vision Guide was designed to keep programming at a minimum, yet allow the application programmer to manipulate vision sequences and objects from SPEL programs. In most applications, the vision sequences created with the point and click interface do most of the work. It is a simple matter to run the sequences from SPEL and use the results to guide the robot, inspect parts, etc. With this type of design, an applications engineer can create sequences without doing any programming before actually designing the application. This can aid in verifying field of view, accuracy, etc. before quoting a job. Then, at design time, he/she can use the sequences that were created in the actual project.

Vision Guide is supported by commands in the SPEL language that runs in the robot controller. After creating vision sequences with the point and click interface, you can run these sequences and get the results from your SPEL programs. You can also read and set properties for vision sequences and objects.

This section contains instructions on how to use Vision Guide commands in SPEL programs.
